core_model.structure_summary: A first-order ordinary differential system for angular variables on a graph, where each unit is driven by an intrinsic rate plus a weighted sum over neighbors of the sine of pairwise angle differences.

variables.meaning: angular variable of unit i on the circle
intrinsic drift rate of unit i
directed, weighted, signed coupling coefficient from unit j to unit i (adjacency/weight matrix, generally asymmetric)

notes: Core is the generalized coupled-phase-oscillator system on a weighted, signed, oriented graph. Equations clearly show the sin(x_i - x_j) phase coupling weighted by a possibly asymmetric matrix a_{ij}; the paper's results concern existence/uniqueness/stability of the phase-locked fixed points. The 1/n mean-field form and the mean-field order-parameter reduction (r,psi) appear as special/derived cases.
